Blasphemous é um dos metroidvanias mais marcantes que já joguei. O jogo cria uma atmosfera sombria e única, com uma pixel art incrível, trilha sonora excelente e um mundo cheio de mistérios.  O ponto mais forte é como a culpa está presente em toda a experiência. Ela molda os personagens, os cenários e a própria história, tornando Cvstodia um lugar fascinante e perturbador ao mesmo tempo.  O combate é desafiador, a exploração recompensa a curiosidade e a ambientação é simplesmente inesquecível. Recomendo muito para quem gosta de metroidvanias e mundos sombrios. 10/10

A dark and atmospheric metroidvania with fantastic pixel art, rewarding exploration, and a fascinating world to uncover. The game rarely explains everything directly, making discoveries, hidden quests, and pieces of lore feel genuinely satisfying to find.  The combat is challenging but fair, and most deaths feel like the result of your own mistakes rather than poor design. While some platforming sections and bosses can be frustrating, the sense of progression and discovery consistently makes the journey worthwhile.  A memorable experience that's easy to recommend to anyone who enjoys challenging combat, interconnected exploration, and rich worldbuilding.

I would recommend this game on the art alone. It's stunning, inspiring, dare I say metal af. Every enemy design, every NPC, every detail in the background is worth stopping and admiring. This is one of those games that transcends beyond "good graphics" into absolute art. And the rest of the aspects of the game are just as stellar. The exploration is open ended and rewarding, the combat is simple but chunky and satisfying, the music is subtle but driving. The overall pacing was perfect, it did and said a ton mechanically and narratively without overstaying its welcome. I could've done with more complex movement and combat, which would have allowed more complex enemy encounters and allowed for more skill expression. Regardless, the unique kill animations still made me go :O every time so that makes up for it I guess.

Let's be honest, everyone whom bought Blasphemous did it for the beautiful pixel art and its brutal theme. Can't blame ya! I was one of those people as well. It totally delivered on this front, I think it is the most impressive pixel art I've ever seen, perfectly complimented by the amazing art direction and theming. The game has great voice acting, deep and cryptic lore, with an expansive and varied world to explore. Just this alone could convince some people to absolutely love it and it's definitely a big selling point for the game.  It's a Metroidvania at it's core, which it does pretty well, but I have some gripes to discuss. I didn't like the amount of backtracking I had to do in this game. This is due to the the lack of handholding and somewhat cryptic objectives in the game. This can be seen as a positive by the true Metroidvania fans out there, but as a casual dibble-dabbler of the genre, it became mildly frustrating at times. The game also commonly dangles unreachable collectibles in front of the player, some of which are important to progression. With the right items or pathways unlocked, these obviously become obtainable, but this forces the player to regularly backtrack and keep track of where the missed collectibles are on the map at all times. Combine this with only being able to teleport to certain locations on the map and you have a reasonable explanation of why it could be a frustrating experience. Luckily, when you do manage to progress in the game, there is a ton of fun to be had and the level design is overall very solid. I would say the platforming is about average, it doesn't do anything new or exciting, but it is commendable.  The combat is solid all be it a bit too basic. There is a generic 3-hit combo and an aerial hit, both using the sword. To make combat interesting, you need to either dash, jump or block your way through the enemies. Combine this with the varied enemy designs, each with their own strengths, weaknesses and patterns, you have yourself a solid combat system. There is a skill tree of sorts that allows the player to unlock new abilities as they progress, but I found it to be quite lacking and uninteresting making the game feel the same the whole way through. With the core of the combat being so solid, there is no issue I take with what is there, but I definitely won't go out on a limb and say the combat is anything to be too excited about. The game also features a wide range of bosses which are all an epic spectacle. The difficulty is manageable throughout, if you are good at games you might be able to defeat most bosses and areas with one or two deaths only.  So overall, I think we have a really solid game packaged in an intriguing and beautiful world. While the combat, platforming and progression systems are all fairly basic and don't do much to push the genre forward, they come together to create a deep and rewarding Metroidvania experience.

Disclaimer, I have not finished the game, but I believe I am close, I just want to put something out before I possibly forget to finish the game.  I'll get what I like out first - the aesthetic and art are first and foremost the best parts of this game. It has beautiful pixel art with horrific bosses that are creative in design, and I wish there were more games that drew inspiration from catholicism. -the combat is usually satisfying, each sword slash feels meaty and, while the block window is generous, it still feels good to pull it off. And the bosses have fun and interesting mechanics to have to deal with, which a lot of games don't quite get. -the glory kills are fun to pull off a few times, don't remember what they replenish or give the player if anything. -making the player not lose currency upon not being able to reclaim their spirit after death does mean you won't get set back from losing currency, but even then, this really only applies to low level gamers or first timers for souls-likes.  Now for what I don't like as much: - the platforming feels repetitive. For as long as I've played, I don't believe any new platforming mechanics have been introduced yet. Sure there are different hazards that you have to avoid in each area, but there is nothing enhancing player mobility. Now, I'm not asking for the same exact mobility options that all metroidvanias seem to copy from one another, but I wish there was just one or two more mechanics added midgame to spice it up to keep the platforming from getting dry. Afterall, platforming is one of the main mechanics that makes this genre. - I am not opposed to spiked pits in game, but there have been a few times where I just want to move and I end up dropping down onto a pit of spikes that were off screen. I think this also bleeds into my previous complaint as because of traps like this, you have to take platforming slow at times so as to avoid instant deaths  which I think makes the platforming feel like even more of a slog. - it's common place to have a quick travel in all sorts of genres for good reason. While I don't mind backtracking as it is part of metroidvanias that make them non-linear and differentiates their gameplay, there is only so much backtracking a player is willing to do. So as more of the map is discovered, quick travel spots are discovered to help the player cut down on backtracking through areas they've been through a million times before. However, a key aspect of these quick travel points is location. You mainly want them to be in areas in which different branching paths are or where points of interest are. The issue in blasphemous is that the quick travel portals are out of the way of the player, usually making them a longer or more dangerous route than just traversing your way to where you want to go. I have probably only actually used the quick travel portals once or twice just to get back to the center of the map. I am dreading having to go back to previous areas because there isn't going to be any easy way to do it and that is why I'm afraid I won't finish this game even this close. - there are also some sections where the hazards feel like bullcrap, like you are going to take some damage unless you are the best player ever. I'm not complaining about difficulty cause I've played multiple souls-likes at their intended difficulty, I'm just complaining of unfair/unreasonable difficulty. -Souls-likes usually are known for story that is very complex but at the same time, not being in your face. For most it's fun to maybe piece together a little here or there to understand it and then deep dive into it later to fully appreciate the world. It feels like Blasphemous is trying to give you the deeper lore upfront to try and world build, but this usually results in long passages or monologues in old english that tends to confuse me or go over my head. While the story is interesting, I found it more enjoyable and engaging when I could actually understand it reading it off of the wiki instead of having it forced upon me confusingly.   End summary: It is not a bad metroidvania, I have enjoyed my time with it for the most part. I just think there are some glaring issues which hold it back from its true potential. I still want to finish it to see the game through, but it's going to be hard to bring myself to do it (part of the reason why I wrote this review was to do something other than play it while engaging with it so as to feel somewhat productive). I don't think I will be playing it again if I finish it, and I doubt I will ever play the sequel. If you are a hardcore metroidvania fan, you could try Blasphemous and maybe still like it a lot despite its flaws, but I'm probably a moderate metroidvania fan who likes the genre and has played a decent amount of the genre, but it is hard for me to continue one with bigger flaws.

It's pretty good. Stylistically, I like it more than other titles in the same genre. But how does the gameplay feel?  The Penitent One controls nicely. He's weighty in both his mobility and combat. Parries feel great, his attacks have a good flow, and he moves in a very expected manner. But you are completely useless in the air and against most projectiles. You also don't gain fervour fast enough to use the abilities a lot. Also I wish the Penitent One had a slightly larger sword.  I didn't find the game particularly difficult. Again, moments that require you to be in the air are you at your weakest. The platforming can be kinda tricky but that's mostly due to grabbing ledges and the wall climb.  I'm not a fan of the Dark Souls-inspired item descriptions for lore. This is doubled because many items have two descriptions. The world being inspired by a complex relationship with an Abrahamic-inspired religion in itself is very compelling and interesting. I just wish I could learn the Penitent One's purpose in the game world easier.  The base game's length seems under 10 hours which I stretched in trying to complete the map. But it felt very satisfying due to the sheer density of level design, exploration, and number of collectibles. You're rewarded for exploring throughout the map.

상당히 불쾌한 수작.  게임 자체는 재밌다. 하지만 중2병스러운 전혀 알아들을 수 없는 그들만의 난해한 스토리는 지구의 언어로 이해할 수 없는 외계어 수준이다. 그리고 게임 진행을 가장 방해하는 요소로 길찾기를 대표적으로 들 수 있는데, 한번에 클리어 되는 부분이 없고 여기 왔다 저기 갔다 맵 처음과 끝을 반복해서 걸어다니며 구석구석 단서를 찾아야 하는 것은 고통의 연속이다. 예를 들어, 아직 스킬을 배우지 못해서, 아이템을 얻지 못해서, 누군가를 만나지 못해서 다시 맵 여기저기의 함정 맵을 뚫고 길을 찾는 일은 매우 힘든 노동이다. 단순히 맵 하나하나를 다시 찾는 정도가 아니라 머리를 써서 함정을 돌파하고 거기다 컨트롤까지 해야만 하는 일이기 때문에 막힌 길을 돌아서 다시 처음으로 되돌아 가는 것은 여간 귀찮은 일이 아니다. 하지만  이런 요소를 제외한다면, 도트 감성과 그로테스크한 다크판타지 세계관이 잘 섞인 상당히 잘 만든 게임이라고 생각한다. 장점도 크고 재미도 확실히 있다. 하지만 단점도 너무 크다는 것이 문제다. 단순히 플레이 타임을 붙잡기 위해서 맵의 여러군데를 매번 막아놓아서 다시 돌아가야 하는 상황이 한두번이 아니라 항상 벌어지기 때문에 새로운 맵을 뚫을 때마다 왔던 길을 돌아가야 하는 것에 대한 스트레스로 화가 날 지경이다. 마치 게임을 만든 제작진이 제발 우리의 노력을 알아봐주고 맵의 요소 하나하나 꼼꼼히 탐험해줘 라고 강제적으로 말하는 듯한 이 억지 길막은, 제작의 고생을 유저에게 전가하는 듯한 제작진의 의도가 강하게 느껴진다. 이러한 강압적인 길막은 오히려 심각한 마이너스 요소였으며, 이걸로 유저들의 플레이 타임을 붙잡아 놓는다고 해서 갓겜이 되는 것은 아니다.   결론 : 양날의 검같은, 동전의 양면이 동시에 일어나는 이 양자역학같은 게임은 재미도 스트레스도 확실하게 준다.

Ich will das Spiel ja wirklich mögen als einer der Metroidvania Spiele wie Ender Lilies, Hollow Knight, oder sonstiges liebt. Die Atmosphäre passt wirklich gut, das Setting interessant und spannend und die ganze Gameplay Idee echt nicht Schlecht. Aber...es gibt einfach einen Großteil an Punkten die ich einfach nicht richtig durchdacht oder geprüft finde. Das Movement ist für ein Metroidvania einfach nicht gut. Das springen und an Plattformen festhalten ist ein einziger Krampf das dodgen funktioniert manchmal gut manchmal gar nicht ebenso wie der dodge angriff. Die Gebiete bzw. Gegner sind einfach zu unbalanced. Bevor man jetzt sagt "Skill Issue" muss ich sagen es gibt einen unterschied zwischen Schwer und nervig im gegensatz zu den Bossen die alle wirklich einfach sind und selten mehr als nur einen Versuch brauchen. Gegner die nur einen oder manchmal zwei Angriffe spammen sehe ich nicht wirklich als Schwer aber in manchen Situationen super unnötig. Vor allem weil es 90% immer die Fernkampf Gegner sind die sich entweder Teleportieren, schneller angreifen als du laufen oder ausweichen kannst oder Gegner die an Positionen stehen an denen du nichts oder recht wenig machen kannst da sie in irgendeinem "Gebiets Gimmik" stehen. Die Gegner fühlen sich gesamt eher langweilig an. Die Designs sind klasse. Aber die Angriffe lieblos und Basic. Die Gegner sind auch echt häufig recyclet. Die Gebiete und Map große Klasse. Einfallsreich und Abwechselnd. Das einzige was ich nicht mag sind die "Gebirts Gimmiks" Fast jedes Gebiet hat ein bestimmtes Gimmik. Zum Beispiel, Gift dass von oben runtertropft und Schaden macht, Wind der die Richtung ändert und je nach dem dich beschleunigt oder Verlangsamt. Oder Kettenblitze die in bestimmte Richtungen schießen im Sekundentakt. Wenn da jetzt noch einer dieser nervigen Fernkampf Gegner drinsteht kann das ziemlich schnell einfach nur nervig und frustrierend sein. Aber auch hier, nicht jedes Gimmik ist schlecht. Nur manche auch wieder einfallslos. Du steht an einer bestimmten stelle, bekommst schaden. Also wenn ich das Spiel bewerten müsste 2/5 Sternen, gibt bessere Metroidvanias als das. Schade eigentlich.  English: I really want to like this game, given that I love Metroidvanias like *Ender Lilies*, *Hollow Knight*, and the like. The atmosphere is spot-on, the setting is interesting and exciting, and the core gameplay concept isn't bad at all. But... there are just so many aspects that feel poorly thought-out or untested. The movement simply isn't good for a Metroidvania; jumping and grabbing onto platforms is a total hassle, and dodging—along with the dodge attack—works sometimes but fails at others. The areas and enemies are just too unbalanced. Before anyone cries "skill issue," let me say there’s a difference between "hard" and "annoying"—especially compared to the bosses, which are all really easy and rarely take more than one attempt. I don't consider enemies that spam just one or two attacks to be "hard," just incredibly tedious in certain situations. This is especially true for the ranged enemies—which make up 90% of the problem—that either teleport, attack faster than you can run or dodge, or stand in spots where you can do little to nothing because they're positioned within some "area gimmick." Overall, the enemies feel rather dull; the designs are great, but the attacks are uninspired and basic. They also get recycled way too often. The areas and map design, however, are top-notch—inventive and varied. The only thing I dislike is the "area gimmicks." Almost every zone has a specific gimmick: for instance, poison dripping from above that deals damage, wind that shifts direction to speed you up or slow you down, or chain lightning firing in specific directions every second. Throw one of those annoying ranged enemies into the mix, and things can quickly become irritating and frustrating. That said, not every gimmick is bad—though some are uninspired. You just stand in a certain spot and take damage. If I had to rate the game, I'd give it 2 out of 5 stars; there are better Metroidvanias out there. It's a shame, really.

I enjoyed Blasphemous a lot. I really liked the dark fantasy setting, the lore, and the combat. The pixel art graphics are amazing and help create a dark and unique atmosphere. The world feels interesting to explore, and I enjoyed discovering more about the story and its characters.  One of my favorite things was the finishing moves on enemies. They look great, feel satisfying to pull off, and fit the game's brutal style perfectly. I also liked the puzzles, which add some variety and make exploration more rewarding.  The game is challenging, but I didn't find it too difficult overall. Most of the time it feels fair, and it only gets really tough if you're underleveled or missing important upgrades or key items.  Overall, I had a great time with Blasphemous. It combines great combat, interesting lore, beautiful pixel art, and fun exploration into a really memorable experience. I can't wait to play Blasphemous!

De todos los tipo souls que he jugado, el unico que no es de FromSoft pero logra capturar la atmosfera de Dark Souls a la perfección es BLASPHEMOUS. Además es que no considero que esté imitando ni copiando esa atmósfera, sino que la recupera desde sus propias raíces. Sus escenarios, su arquitectura, sus símbolos religiosos y los bosses no parecen una interpretación exótica de una cultura ajena, sino una expresión auténtica de una tradición que forma parte de su propia historia. (Además las cinematicas le dan mil vueltas a las del blasphemous 2)

esse jogo é simplesmente absurdo de bom. Eu adoro temas como eles colocaram em blasphemous e saber o processo criativo do jogo deixa ele ainda mais absurdo (no melhor sentido possível). As referencias são incríveis, é divertido ler os itens que você pega durante o jogo, é divertido saber a historia dos personagens por mais "irrelevantes" que eles sejam. A quantidade de coisas secretas torna o jogo muito interessante na hora da exploração, vc fica que nem tonto batendo nas paredes para ver algo secreto KKKKKKKK. As mecânicas de combate são criativas e tudo é muito lindo, pra mim é o ponto mais alto de todo o jogo, blasphemous é lindo, e quando vc  vê que tudo tem ao menos um significado naquele mundo, por mais miserável que seja, deixa tudo mais poético e incrível. Não achei que ia gostar do jogo por juntar metroidvania e souslike, e ainda bem que eu estava errada, virou um dos meus jogos favoritos. Obg.

三周目，红蓝忏悔已过，完成三结局，总体来看，较为推荐入手（6/10），如果你是忠实的银河恶魔城玩家，推荐入手（7.5/10）。 先说优点，较为优秀的地图设计，独特的剧情和画风都非常加分，真的十分建议大家好好了解一下剧情，每一个boss的剧情都有背景和引申，甚至每一个物品都有小故事，做的非常不错，除此之外，个别boss交互性很不错（点名表扬一阶段克兰莎，学会格挡之后很爽），除几个boss，大部分的难度都不高，很容易几遍过 接下来是缺点，首先就是稀烂的手感，主角残疾人一样的性能（从头到无二段跳），本作的地形杀较为恶心（但最主要的原因还是主角是残疾人和手感问题）；然后就是boss，前面说了boss难度不高，因为大部分boss的攻击种类都不算多；再有就是人物倒地没有无敌帧，任何人（包括小怪）一肘就起飞，和纸片人一样 最后着重点名表扬血污联动跳跳乐，如果你觉得生活暗淡无光甚至想不开，建议你去试试，配上shi一样的操作手感，苦痛之路在他面前都算弟弟  然后是给想要成就的或是想试试的玩家的一些建议： 首先不算开局的boss，打赢第一个boss后，建议你们去教会捐款，2k：赎罪功能   5k：一个念珠   1w；存档回蓝    2w：存档点传送   3w：音效   5w：一个念珠   10w：一个皮肤 物品描述用h和y上下滑动，w和s（方向上下）进行选择 然后是镇魂曲成就，需要本体全部boss，包括出生点boss，羊头，圣母，锤哥，三姐妹，大主教，弃婴，火男，克兰莎（一阶段即可），圣王（双阶段的即可） 对于树人任务，只要你不和他对话，就可以先打其他boss，然后再去找他，一旦对话，任务就算开始，就只能打圣母boss（前期不是很推荐，因为雪山对于前期来说，很阴间）。 还有正义天罚，就是弹反后，打到怪物的一瞬间前按攻击键，成功后有红色闪电特效，多试试还是很容易成功的 想完成真结局的玩家，建议去看看攻略  最后给想多周目的玩家一些个人建议，多周目建议先选红忏悔，然后再蓝，三周目及之后的强度与二周目一致

A very solid take on a metroidvania style game with a little bit of Dark Souls thrown in the mix. Combat is simple yet challenging at times. Taking the time to master your kit is very much a requirement, you won't be blundering your way through this game unless you take the time to master the skills given to you. This is my second attempt at the game, the first time I played it I didn't get very far after the first boss. I kept making mistakes and I came to the conclusion that the game was too hard for me.  But here I am back at it about 12 hours into my current save file and I'm doing much better than I originally had. I will say that Blasphemous doesn't do a very good job in directing you where to go. There are a few times where I was just outright lost and had absolutely no idea where to go (much like my experiences with Dark Souls.) But with the few google searches I have done on my playthrough I was able to get myself once again going in the right direction without having to really spoil anything.  I can for sure say that this game is very much worth your time. It's also got a pretty reasonable price. That's on top of the fact that every single DLC aside from the OST and a cosmetic skin has been free. Buy this game and play it, I don't think you will be disappointed at all unless of course you don't like metroidvania games in the first place.

Blasphemous is one of the most unforgettable games I’ve played. Its dark, gothic world feels oppressive, mysterious, and beautiful at the same time. Every location in the land of Blasphemous is packed with haunting religious imagery, towering cathedrals, decaying statues, twisted miracles, and disturbing creatures that make the world feel ancient and cursed. The pixel art is exceptional; every frame is filled with intricate details, from the texture of stone walls and rusted armor to the grotesque designs of bosses and enemies. The atmosphere is constantly enhanced by melancholic music, distant chants, and ambient sounds that create a sense of loneliness and reverence. Combat feels weighty and brutal, making every victory satisfying and every mistake costly. What truly sets the game apart is its storytelling—rather than explaining everything directly, it invites players to uncover its lore through exploration, item descriptions, and environmental details. The result is a world that feels alive with history, suffering, and mystery. Blasphemous is not just a Metroidvania; it is a work of dark art that combines stunning visuals, rich symbolism, challenging gameplay, and masterful world-building into an experience that lingers in the mind long after the credits roll.

As an atmospheric connoisseur, this has been one of the solidest additions to my little library. The pixel art is a literal masterpiece! Every boss design and gothic environment makes it feel like an interactive art gallery. While the combat feel and brutal executions are extremely fun, it requires serious effort and mental dedication. However, the post boss hand tremors and the ultimate sense of accomplishment make it all worth it. The only catch is that the heavy religious allegories and guilt themes mildly triggered my childhood religious trauma. At one point, I found myself randomly repenting for my sins. Also, listening to gregorian chants in the background while playing hits incredibly different and turns the atmosphere up to eleven. (I recommend it to try) If you have the patience and an eye for high quality visuals don't miss out. 9/10.

[h1]Discreto ma costoso[/h1] Salve a tutti, scrivo questa recensione in data 02/06/2026 alle ore 11:13 dopo aver accumulato circa 50 ore di gioco, platinato lo stesso e quindi avendo una portata di opinione importante. Partirei dal costo del videogioco, completamente sbagliato e orgoglioso, stiamo parlando a prezzo pieno di ben 25 EUROZZI, quasi il costo di un the witcher 3 o di un read dead redemption in offerta, ma di cosa stiamo parlando?? questo gioco secondo me vale tra i 7 euro e i 12 euro, quindi non vale minimamente ma neanche LONTANAMENTE tutti questi soldi, e già quindi si nota un'avidità degli sviluppatori importante; io per fortuna l'ho preso in sconto a 5€ perchè se no veramente mi buttavo da un vulcano mezzo nudo. Andrei ora sulla trama...che dire...una sorta di brutta copia di dark souls, cioè non si capisce niente del mondo di gioco, dei nemici, dei boss, dei personaggi...i risvolti narrativi sono frivoli se non assenti...ma la cosa peggiore secondo me è l'assenza di un sistema funzionale di dettagli e scrittura; trovi delle ossa che raccontano la storia di personaggi COMPLETAMENTE inutili ma non trovi dettagli importanti sui boss che devi sconfiggere, il videogioco è vuoto e privo di contenuto in questo senso, apprezzo però che almeno ti venga detto cosa fare e dove, a differenza di un dark souls appunto. Il comparto grafico è becero, grafica a pixel bruttina e non ai livelli di un Kingdom Two Crowns dove tutto è magnificamente perfetto e lavorato, quindi penso che in questo gioco sia una scusa per non metterci una grafica fatta bene, però non è nemmeno così brutta, si nota l'impegno nei video scena per esempio...ma alla fine della fiera non conviene tanto. La giocabilità invece mi è piaciuta molto, persiste una grande dinamicità tra abilità e sistemi di combattimento, riflessi di tempismo con parate e schivate, strategie memoriche nell'apprendere i nemici, Interessanti meccanismi di piattaforme e salti, quindi su questo mi reputo molto contento. Musiche....madre teresa...veramente superficiali e banali, il reparto musicale è il cornetto di fabbrica a 1,19€ che mangi nel turno di mattina di lavoro mentre piangi in auto, una robba...non ti dico orrenda però....di una scontatezza molto triste. Le sfide del gioco e quindi gli achivements sono disgustosi e perditempo, un platino disonesto il mio fatto con noia e illegalità, il mio organo riproduttivo è stato ammosciato non poco. Organizzazione di gioco inizialmente perfetta ma poi ti perdi miseramente, troppi segreti nascosti in maniera fraudolenta, troppa lentezza con i ritmi di gioco, avanti e indietro continui noiosi, missioni secondarie a volte malfunzionanti come quella della chiave del bibliotecario, e altre schifezze qua e là. Finale che mi ha deluso tantissimo, uno dei peggiori finali mai visti in un videogioco. Personaggi secondari carini, ma anch'essi confusionari e distorti, semplicemente hanno dei dialoghi interessanti e delle grafiche intriganti. E nulla...sto gioco è un panino vuoto, cioè non è che sia una schifezza totale ma dai social avevo letto una sorta di opera d'arte nascosta...però questo videogioco per me...sommato il tutto, è anche gravemente insufficiente soprattutto per colpa del prezzo che è come un kebab in un ristorante italiano, completamente fuoriluogo e sbagliato. Dunque concludo dicendo che per me questo gioco merita un 5-/10, qualcosa del genere. Grazie della lettura, bella.

Hermoso juego nada más que decir; sobre todo en el apartado artístico, banda sonora y elenco de voces en español.  Tiene una historia amplia y muchos secretos que se pueden leer en los ítems y demás pero depende de ti si quieres leer cada texto.   Los Bosses son muy interesantes, el único detalle es que pueden ser algo fáciles o predecibles EXCEPTO por un personaje que es el jefe mas retador del juego que es Isidora y la pase muy bien sufriendo muchas muertes pero finalmente aprendiendo su patrón de ataque (boss opcional).   Debido a la gran cantidad de secretos, en el tema de la exploración si puede ser algo tedioso, sobre todo con los portales y también porque hay un secreto en la iglesia que al dar cierta cantidad de diezmo te desbloquea el poder viajar entre los checkpoints. Esto lo supe por un tutorial, de no haberlo sabido hubiese sido mas estresante la exploración para mi.   En el tema de la jugabilidad y el combate, creo que es algo que se tiene que pulir mejor no se siente tan fluido o con versatilidad como otros metrolvania, hay ataques que en realidad casi no se utilizan como el de atacar en picada y el ataque que puedes sostenerte unos seg en el aire mientras atacas un enemigo u objeto, no lo sentí tan fluido tampoco o satisfactorio para usarlo seguido.   En general, es un juego muy bueno. Creo que se disfruta aún más si creciste en una cultura con una fuerte influencia cristiana o católica, ya que muchas de sus referencias y simbolismos resultan familiares.

Blasphemous was the first metroidvania and soulslike game that I ever played. And I AM THRILLED.  Spanish gothic aesthetics with a mysterious plot that unravels up until the last second was not something I expected going into this. Curiosity of pixel graphics brought me in, but platforming, combat, puzzles and rewards are what me stay... NAY, made me want to play it again. The rerun itself was such a breath of fresh air, because of the game experience was so different the second time around. This game simply would not stop delivering new discoveries, and with different endings and juicy questlines.  The artwork, the music, the aesthetic expression of the creators, coupled with satisfying gameplay makes this game a masterpiece. Really excited about what lies beyond the gates of Blasphemous 2 (already installed).

First playthrough was overall good and I enjoyed some of the boss fights.  I felt the difficulty level was uneven, sometimes it spiked to very hard and other times it was too easy.  I found the platforming elements tedious and frustrating at times, as you can die instantly when knocked into a spike pit or into a bottomless pit.  The common enemies were at times more challengin than the bosses because they could swarm you, or keep knocking you down with no way to move.  I beat some of the bosses first try, and some second try.  There were only a few I had to try many times, such as [spoiler]the red guard with a hammer, the female knight with sword, the singing woman in the ossuary and the snake in the pirate area, and the final boss.  However, most of these were a lot easier once I got all Mea Culpa upgrades and some of the better rosary beads and health upgrades.[/spoiler] The item finding missions made no sense, and it was difficult to remember where everyone was, but the map and custom markers were useful for this.  I ended up with some unused key items as it was unclear that progressing past a certain point would terminate those quests.  I 'm really not a fan of games that do this, and force you to start over to complete a side quest.  It was a case of trial and error and most often I would give random items to people without realising I was meant to give the item to them because the explanations were so vague.   Second playthorough I rage quit because the added difficulty and handicap made the game unenjoyable, which is a shame as I'll likely not get to see all the hidden bosses and alternative endings.  I have completed games which were technically more difficult than this, but I don't have the motivation to complete this several more times.  I also didn't like the ladder grab mechanic on some of the time trial levels; it just doesn't work reliably enough to put it into a timed challenge like that.   If you just want to do a single playthrough, it's worth it when it's on offer, but new game plus isn't for me.

Un sobresaliente "metroidvania", con una elección artística bastante original, una buena jugabilidad, un "OST" brillante y un magnifico diseño en "pixel-art". Presentado en una historia intrigante y oscura, inspirada en el folclore y la religión española. Puntos a favor: -Buen diseño de niveles. -Original en su diseño de arte y en su épica de narrativa oscura. -Fácil de jugar, difícil de dominar. -Un muy buen apartado musical y de actuaciones de voz. -Re-jugable y a un precio irresistible. Puntos en contra: -Se desaprovechan las posibilidades, del aspecto jugable en el combate. -La historia y narrativa, pueden tornarse demasiado criptica o confusa. Sin saber por que haces aquello o esto. Dicho lo cual...   -Resolver algunos acertijos o averiguar para que sirven algunos objetos. Pude pasar de muy difícil a directamente imposible. Terminando por consultar una guía. Debido a la vinculación de estos objetos y misterios, con una narrativa que se excede en lo ambiguo o desconcertante. -Se agradece que puedas dejar marcas con distintos símbolos en el mapa (para ubicar sitios u objetos a los que aun no puedas acceder). Pero... Se agradecería aun más: Un contador de coleccionables y un marcador de porcentaje de mapa explorado. Pero no solo en general. Si no de zonas en especifico. Para así evitar estar dando vuelta en círculos, durante horas. En conclusión: Un imprescindible en tu colección de "metroidvanias". Con algunas detalles a pulir, pero con una enorme y disfrutable calidad. Saludos.

This game is mediocre at best. My biggest problem with it is how confusing it is. Even though you can beat the game without specific abilities (relics), getting some of them without a guide is completely impossible. They have extremely cryptic methods of acquisition, which IMO is really annoying.  For example, the Nail Uprooted from Dirt requires you to do a specific quest where you need to find a random NPC called "Redento" five times throughout the game. I talked to him the first time and helped him move a bit, but I didn't speak to him after that. Therefore, the quest didn't continue, even after I explored the whole map and cleared the path he takes, because I didn't do that. Terrible game design.  Another problem I have with this game is that all the bosses (except Isidora, whom I really liked) are extremely easy. The final boss, the happy snake, everyone is easy, and at worst they take like 3 tries. But spikes are evil . For some ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ reason, someone thought it was a great idea to make spikes one-shot you. It's really annoying. The ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ spikes are clearly the final boss of this game.

Acabo de terminar todo lo que el juego me pudo ofrecer y puedo afirmar que este juego es cine. Debo admitir que me costo MUCHISIMO, engancharme al juego al principio, tarde años en decidirme a jugarlo pero cuando le di esa oportunidad lo entendí todo.  - El estilo artístico y visual es increíble, esta basado claramente en el folclore y en la historia española, se entendió lo que quisieron demostrar en el momento en que abrí el juego. Los diseños de la mayoría de los jefes son increíblemente macabros, espeluznantes y tétricos… y eso hace que su historia te llame mas la atención. Se notaron mucho las referencias al manga Berserk de Kentaro Miura, y es hermoso que su nombre este eternizado en una cuenta de este juego.  - La historia es un poco difícil de entender al principio, sobre todo porque la gente no logra entender rápido lo que el milagro es y tampoco quien es Escribar, que cambia mucho de apodos durante la historia del juego. Pero en el momento en el que entendes esas dos cosas ya podes entender absolutamente cualquier quest o historia de personaje.  - Los diseños de los niveles están bastante bien hechos, muchas veces te penalizan por perder haciendo que tengas que recorrer todo un camino de vuelta para pelear contra un jefe. Además de esto, el juego logra hacer que, incluso con temática oscura, cada zona del mapa se sienta extremadamente distinta a las otras.  - Los coleccionables y los quest no son difíciles de encontrar, si prestas atención podes ir consiguiendo todo mientras exploras, pero hay algunas excepciones en ciertos puzles que no se te pasan por la cabeza ni queriendo.  - Lo único de lo que realmente me quejo es... la dificultad. Tal vez es porque soy un enfermo pero me pareció bastante fácil, me estresaba mas la exploración que los jefes, los cuales me tomaban muy pocos intentos. Los únicos jefes que REALMENTE me costaron fueron Crisanta en su tercera fase y ya!  Este juego transmite efectivamente todo lo que quiso transmitir y lo hace de manera perfecta, esta increíblemente bien logrado, lo re recomiendo.   Absolute Blasphemia.

For a platforming game the platforming feels horrible and movement is far from fluid.  Visually you often feel like you should have landed on a platform but you have to be further in to the platform to actually land.  Often leading to fall into insta death spikes or into the void and respawning feeling frustrated.   Half of the enemies are just annoying to deal with, the other half is just 1 hit trash.   Why would you put the effect of the rosary beads at the bottom of the text so you have to scroll down every time you want to see what the items do? I don't really understand why you can only have 3 relics at a time, it just leads to a lot of unnecessary inventory shuffling.   As for bugs, I haven't encountered many but there have been a few times where I wasn't able to use flasks.  As many others have said before, the art direction has a lot going for this game and is partly the only reason I pushed through until the end.

Blasphemous me surpreendeu bastante, não imaginava que um metroidvania/soulsvania seria tão lindo, além de ser um jogo indie ele me conquistou muito com sua narrativa muito fora do comum sobre os aspectos do catolicismo(de uma forma distorcida), a jogabilidade e grind do game são excelentes.  Puzzles e bossfights, sempre tem um segredo pra descobrir, seja em uma parade, no chão, ou interação com quadros/portas, os chefes são diferentes de um aspecto desing, as vezes pode ser um bebê com uma serpente, ou até mesmo um cabeça com uma espada gigante kkkk, mas de certa forma ele tem o seu próprio estilo sem copiar nem um outro do gênero, me pegou muito como o jogo é lindo em gráficos, além de uma ótima ambientação, efeitos sonoros e um bom enredo em cima.  Recomendo bastante pra quem curte esse gênero, principalmente pra quem já jogou, ou gosta de hollow knight, Blasphemous acaba sendo mais fácil do quê o hk, mas não deixa de ter uma ótima dificuldade, vá por mim.  Excelente jogo! Oh Blasphemous, where do I even begin.....  This game changed my life. I love Christian/Catholicism based games or Medieval type games and Metroidvania is got be my favourite genre. It's fun to discover little hidden secrets and fight unsettling bosses. Game design is epic and the game is not that challenging (trust me, I'm not that skilled) and is one of the more easier Souls-like games out there. 美术风格别具一格，但是有点为了难而难了，真的就是只可远观而不可亵玩，有很多地方你不看攻略根本不知道怎么过去，有魂味，但是仅仅是在形式设计上，地图设计看似环环相扣，但其实比较难和粪，游戏的剧情还算不错，毕竟体积这么小的游戏有这么多内容也是难能可贵，boss并不算难，并没有卡住我多少时间反而是地图和小怪有些让人红温，篝火之间不能传送而且又相距甚远，没有开传送门的话又是三百六十五里路，而且由于2D的风格，小怪精英怪之间没有交互感，奖励也十分小气，给的魂基本只能用来学习技能，非常降低人的探索欲望。说了这么多，基本都是缺点，但是为什么是好评呢？emm 可能是因为没有中评吧，优点的话，没什么好说的，类银河恶魔城加上类魂，这两个的优点加起来就是了，比如类银在进入不同房间之前的期待感和惊喜感甚至是解脱感，类魂终于战胜千辛万苦的成就感，其他的中规中矩吧。

Sorry but one issue outweighs all the good things about this game. It has an interesting story, and overall a good game, but absolutely no controller support. I wanted to play through it a second time with an xbox series X controller, which steam says this game supports, but not only are half of the actions (like jumping) not there, the buttons in the settings for controls are numbered 1-9 instead of 'X', 'RB', 'LT' etc., and there is no button to make desired changes. I looked online and this appears to be an issue many people are having, and it wouldn't be a difficult fix, and we all know 2d souls-likes like hollow knight give the best player experience when played on controller. This alone turned this game from one deserving an automatic gleaming recommendation to a frustrated 4/10. Please fix this, then I will change my review.

The Last Faith мне понравилась больше.  Blasphemos очень красивая метроидвания, но после 10 часов прохождения игра начала так неплохо душить бэктрекингом. Вот что я не люблю (аж раздражает) так это куча важных сюжетных предметов, которые надо разносить всем встреченным тобою персонажам по немаленьким регионам игры. Хотелось чтобы в будущем данную проблему искоренили сюжетным маркером (или маячком) для правильного пути, дабы сэкономить время игрока.  По истечении 25 ч так и не смог найти крюк кошку (или что из этого типа) дабы добить все отмеченные места с хватом для неё. Игра была пройдена на "плохую концовку" и изучать мир уже не хотелось от слова совсем.  Но первую половину игру (12 ч - 15) было приятно так искать секреты, скрытые проходы, боссов, амулеты, доп. магические способности - особенно понравился щит в виде 2 шаров, дающих всем урон кто в него попадет. Сюжетные вставки (хоть их и не много) сделаны гротескно и мощно - что аж хочется увидеть анимационный фильм по данной игре в будущем. Шипы, недолюбленные многими игроками, мне в игре особо не доставили какого-нибудь сильного дискомфорта. Саундтрек из струнных и клавишных даёт +50% к прекрасной, мрачной атмосфере игры. Нагоняющий таинственности и не обясняющий всего лор игры неимоверно подстёгивает изучать и читать все описания и предметы в игре. Дизайн и общий арт - это что моно весить на стены в квартиру вашего дома.  Правда, к концу игры у меня были уже смешанные впечатления от прохождения. Смысла от сбора всех предметов не было, всех боссов (как я думаю) я одолел, как и нахождения полезных в боях умений.  Всё же игра дала мне много часов запоминающегося путешествия по мрачным землям Кастодии (в эстетике испанской Инквизиции) и достойна взять почетное место среди представителей данного жанра. Уж слишком игра уникальна и самобытна.

Obra maestra de metroidvania 100% ibérica.  Que el lore esté inspirado en el folclore y cultura religiosa de España (sobre todo de Andalucía) me tiene dando volteretas. Han exprimido lo mejorcito de los elementos de Semana Santa, dándole un toque gótico y penitente (nunca mejor dicho) a la cara más ceñida y restrictiva de la Iglesia.   La ambientación oscura y magnífica de los bosses es digna de admirar, la dialéctica de los NPCs es increíble y su doblaje no deja indiferente a nadie; un acento andaluz potente que encaja a la perfección con su ambientación geográfica y contemporánea.  Me quedo corta con todo lo que tenga que decir de este juego Si has crecido con rechazo hacia la Iglesia, este juego es un extraño proceso de catarsis y redención hacia tu propia religión (siempre que seas cristiano).  JUEGAZO

La combinación perfecta entre Dante's Inferno, Dark Souls y Castlevania. La iconografía católica es aterradora e incómoda, pero al mismo tiempo bella por el estilo gráfico. La jugabilidad es adictiva, los bosses son masivos y creativos, y la música es perfecta. Habiendo probado ambos idiomas, recomiendo jugarlo en castellano. Los actores de voz son espectaculares, mejores que los de habla inglesa. El juego está desarrollado por españoles, así que es lógico jugarlo en este idioma. Mi única crítica es la historia algo vaga. Al igual que con Dark Souls, la historia se infiere por diálogos más de lo que se muestra. Eso sí, conseguir un final bueno es difícil por la amplia gama de posibilidades y consecuencias de tus actos (a veces sin aviso previo) a lo largo de tu gameplay. De todas formas, de los mejores metroidvania de la última década. 8/10

мувмент и боевка вынудили меня написать этот обзор  3 раза я начинал играть в эту игру, и все 3 раза я поражался тому, насколько топорное и неотзывчивое здесь управление. именно поэтому прошел я ее спустя аж 5 лет после покупки  бласфемус - это просто средоточие провальных геймдизайнерских решений в отношении самого персонажа: начиная с отсутствия индикатора перезарядки рывка и заканчивая цепочками вражеских атак, удерживающих игрока в стаггере  игра наказывает за попытку играть не по инструкции, в результате чего игрок боится совершить лишнее действие, а самой надежной стратегией становится аккуратно бить по 2-3 удара в гарантированные окна для атаки  в этом вопросе у меня всегда была однозначная позиция: какими бы хорошими ни были саундтрек, лор, сюжет, атмосфера и стиль игры, покуда ее основной жанр это не визуальная новелла или какое-нибудь интерактивное кино, геймплей должен стоять на первом месте. и в этой игре он в большей степени вызывает фрустрацию, чем удовольствие

Blasmeous is one of the best Metroidvania games out there.  Do not judge this game by its design.  It has some deep Mechanics and some interesting power ups and fun fighting style with good responsive platforming combing all this you will have a very good time playing it. Don’t afraid of the difficulty of this game. It’s not souls like once you learn the mechanics it will become more like an Action game. My minor complaints are the story and world is very interesting but understanding it is hard. I have to watch youtube afterwards to make sense of it.  Some puzzles are hard to grasp to the point “I doubted this is even a puzzle”. Again I have to watch youtube to know what to do. To sum it up this is a Fantastic game with awesome replayability. Believe me you have to try the new game+ modes it will completely change the way how you will play and approach this game. I played Hades game more than 200 hours but by final hours playing it I feel some kind of emptiness due to that I can’t finish it the way I wanted.  But finishing this game completely gave me a sense of satisfaction I don’t know way.  I also don’t know why I was hooked up to this game this much as well!!!

全成就33小时，慢了一点，因为打了无血瓶的克兰莎二阶段以及二周目的无血瓶锤哥，浪费了我很长时间。 [b] 当然了，如果你真的想玩，觉得这游戏很对你的电波，那你不会因为我的差评离开，如果你认为这个游戏可玩可不玩，那我就不建议你来尝试。  至于渎神1，2的游玩顺序，如果你不知道游戏适不适合你，那我还是建议先玩2，再回来玩1，因为2的商业性更高，更容易被大部分人接受，如果你因为2喜欢上了渎神这个系列，那你可以再回来补票。  游戏的优点前任之述备矣，宗教色彩浓厚啊，演出画面好啊，这些我基本都认同，我主要讲一下我给差评的原因[/b]  主要有几点，可能每一项单拿出来都没达到差评的地步，但是加在一起足够让我给它打一个差评了。 1，汉化问题，剧情神人神语还则罢了，毕竟不是所有人都想听懂你的谜语人剧情，但是遗物的描述翻译也做的不对吧，很多描述都有问题，很大的影响了游戏体验。 2，键位设计问题，很多人刚开始玩连如何退出游戏，文本怎么向下翻动都需要求助社区，我认为这是设计的失误，或者说键盘适配性上的傲慢。 3，游戏手感，游戏的攻击，滑铲，跳跃的手感全都烂完，非常糟糕，爬墙和普攻的设计逻辑有冲突，虽然都是可以适应的东西，但是玩家能适应不代表问题不存在，这些东西很影响游戏体验。 4，地图设计不合理，我认为有很多地图过于庞大冗长，有些房间设计的多余，应该适当的简洁。 5，雪山真是满满的恶意，这个地方的难度曲线设计不合理，非常劝退新手。（当然后面就没有这样的问题了） 6，各种支线引导做的很糟糕，还有一些很容易断掉，不看攻略很难能想到，很多都是先看了攻略知道了支线，才能注意到游戏对支线的提示。

9/10  Grandísimo juego, una obra de arte visual y narrativa. A nivel de gameplay es bastante bueno, aunque siento que tiene alguna que otra "falla", especialmente en los movimientos más complejos del plataformeo (aunque lo coloco así porque desconozco si es tema de mi mando o de la misma configuración de input del juego). A nivel de gameplay, el juego goza con una dificultad bastante normal; salvo algunos jefes del tramo final, el juego es bastante sencillo a nivel de gameplay, aunque recomiendo quizás consultar una que otra guía por si quieres hacer alguna quest, ya que el juego es bastante críptico. Además, se cuenta con un doblaje al castellano espectacular, que hace la aventura mucho más disfrutable, ya que empapa mucho más esta visión cultural de la cual se nutre mucho el videojuego español.  P. D.: No sé si seré el único al que el juego le suele sufrir en algunas ocasiones de problemas de rendimiento, caídas de frames más que nada, y es raro debido a que es el único juego con el que me pasa, pero lo coloco acá porque no sé si seré yo o es el juego.

披着类银皮的但实际没有类银游戏那种“容易看出如何找新能力的线索”的，赤裸裸展示制作组折磨玩家恶趣味的，某些桥段为了血腥恶心而让人生理不适的，主角招式花样多但是怪物不让你发挥所以只能让主角选择轮椅技能的，游戏进度推进需要某些关键道具但是关键道具的用法压根不说或者特别晦涩难懂的，整天念叨“罪、罚、恶”等宗教词汇整的玩家心理不适的。         我说白了这游戏小众一定有它妈了个逼的道理。制作组喜欢折磨，部分受众喜欢被折磨。让这两拨人互相小众互动去吧。 中途的与《血污》游戏的联动，联动人物让我当时一下就觉得不是“神之亵渎”这个游戏的人物，一定是别的游戏的，因为不可能长得这么正常。         任何在这条评价下面留言杠我的、反对我的、让我闭嘴的玩家都没爹妈。发评价是我的言论自由。你可以单独发一个好评价来逐个反驳我的每一条坏评价，你单独反驳我“魂游游戏水平菜”、“不喜欢宗教领域特点游戏可以不玩”、“不好这口像素血腥那就别玩”、“魂游的特点就是晦涩难懂你看不懂就别玩”这些的人，我操你们妈。天天拥护个游戏这拥护个游戏那，我又没骂你自己骂的是游戏，我它妈招你惹你了。         喜欢像素风格的类银游戏而闻名来玩这个游戏但是还没买的玩家，推荐你们去玩《银河战士》SFC版本的老游戏。喜欢打怪战斗BOSS而闻名来玩这个游戏但是还没买的玩家，推荐你们去玩“FS制作组”制作的魂游系列，不要尝试这种“类魂”。

Shortly after beating Esdras, a boss you fight at the end of what seems to be act 1 of this game, I asked myself: "am I actually having fun?" I then realized the answer was: "no, not really."   I thought Esdras was particularly annoying to slog through, but I also realized that even though there were some good moments in the game for sure, overall I was finding it to be more tedious than actually compelling for me more often than not.   A shame since it has phenomenal art direction and solid voice acting and the lore is quite compelling and yeah, even several components of the gameplay and level design are solid. But again, if as a whole I'm just not really having an especially good time with the game the majority of the time I'm playing it then, well...why bother?   Maybe I'll come back to it again someday, but for now I think I'm over this game.

Explorar Cvstodia foi uma experiência incrível, cada área tem muita personalidade, segredos e uma ambientação pesada e marcante.  Passei muita raiva em alguns bosses kkk, teve pelo menos dois que morri inúmeras vezes, mas depois que você aprende o timing dos movimentos e os padrões tudo fica extremamente satisfatório. O combate recompensa muito o aprendizado e a paciência.  A exploração foi uma das coisas que mais gostei, principalmente a sensação clássica de voltar em áreas antigas depois de conseguir novas habilidades e descobrir caminhos novos. O mapa é cheio de detalhes e realmente dá aquela sensação de aventura e descoberta.  Essa ainda foi minha primeira jogada, ainda tenho coisas para fazer e conteúdo da DLC para explorar, mas no geral minha experiência foi excelente. Sem dúvidas Blasphemous merece estar entre os melhores metroidvanias.

[h3]• Une aventure bénie par la souffrance… [/h3]  Blasphemous, ce n’est pas un jeu. C’est une punition divine en pixel art ! Dès les premières minutes, le jeu vous fait comprendre une chose : ici, la douleur fait partie du voyage… et à force, on finit presque par l’apprécier. Presque.  L’univers est exceptionnel : une ambiance religieuse sombre, malsaine et fascinante, portée par une direction artistique pixelisée absolument superbe. Chaque décor respire la souffrance, le sang et la culpabilité. Bref, des vacances très réussies…   Le gameplay assume totalement son inspiration Souls-like : combats exigeants, esquives précises, boss mémorables et quêtes obscures que vous pouvez rater sans même comprendre ce que vous avez raté. Les PNJ peuvent mourir, certaines rencontres sont manquables, et l’histoire se découvre comme un puzzle mystique écrit après trois nuits sans sommeil dans l’ombre des ténèbres. Très Dark Souls dans l’esprit vous me direz.   [h3]⤷ En conclusion : [/h3]  Blasphemous est un excellent Souls-like 2D : beau, brutal, étrange et profondément marquant. Je recommande vivement à tous ceux qui aiment les défis corsés, les univers sombres… et souffrir volontairement pendant leurs heures de détente !

⭐ 8/10 - 40h - SteamDeck  Blasphemous, c'est l'une des meilleures claques artistiques que j'ai pris depuis longtemps dans un metroidvania. L'ambiance est absolument unique : un univers sombre, presque cauchemardesque, qui mêle iconographie catholique espagnole et horreur baroque. On aime ou on déteste, mais moi j'ai adoré. Chaque zone dégage quelque chose d'oppressant et de fascinant à la fois, et la direction artistique est une réussite quasi totale.  Le gameplay est solide. Les combats sont précis, punitifs comme il se doit, et les boss sont mémorables. Ça ne réinvente pas la roue du genre, mais ça le fait avec sérieux et efficacité. La progression est bien ficelée, on débloque toujours quelque chose d'intéressant.  Par contre... le 100 % m'a donné quelques cheveux blancs. Certains objets et secrets sont cachés de manière absolument obscure, parfois derrière des interactions qui ne sont indiquées nulle part. Sans guide, bonne chance pour tout trouver par vous-même. C'est le genre de design qui peut frustrer autant qu'il peut charmer les acharnés de la complétion.  Malgré ce bémol, Blasphemous reste une expérience forte, une véritable œuvre d'auteur dans un genre souvent trop lisse. Si vous aimez l'ambiance pesante, la pixel art soignée et les défis qui ont du mordant, foncez

[b] Cruauté et Sacrifices [/b]  Blasphemous est une ode aux Souls. Une histoire tragique, au milieu d'un monde violent et sanguinaire, accompagné d'un gameplay punitif et impitoyable.  Le jeu est avant tout magnifique visuellement et auditivement. Le pixel art est sublime, chaque animation rend super bien (surtout les exécutions). Les ost sont également du miel pour les oreilles. La musique donne le ton pour chaque biome et renforce l'immersion. L'ambiance du jeu est principalement mis en avant par ses musiques.  L'histoire de Blasphemous est captivante. On nous dépeint un monde détruit et corrompu. On nous vend une quête héroïque et libératrice, tout en étant nuancée. Des objectifs, des regrets, des fiertés... Le jeu nous propose tout ça sur un plateau, mais sans nous laisser y retirer le couvercle... Car aussi passionnants peuvent sembler le scénario et le lore de Blasphemous, ils sont en réalité trop cryptique et difficile à découvrir. On se perd facilement dans la trame principale et le récit est dissimulé derrière des messages complexes et tordue.  De même, le jeu est complexe dans sa globalité. Toutes les quêtes du jeu n'ont que très peu d'indication. Aucun de point d'intérêt n'est présent sur le carte, aucun indice venant de pnj ou autre... Même avec le wiki sous les yeux, certaines quêtes sont complexe à faire. Le level design est assez intéressant par certains endroits mais totalement aléatoire à d'autres, rendant certains objets lié à des quêtes presque impossible à trouver par soit même.  En revanche, le gameplay du jeu est assez agréable. Bien qu'il manque de profondeur et que les contrôles sans un peu rigide, les combats sont très satisfaisant. Les bosses sont très originaux, chacun possède une mécanique propre et souvent très intéressante qui les rendront mémorables. Le jeu propose une grande liberté dans les builds qui ont un réel impact sur le gameplay et permettent d'opter pour le style de votre choix. Cependant, on notera tout de même que les hitbox sont inconstantes et que les phases de plateformes sont strictes et frustrantes.  De même, le jeu comporte pas mal de bugs. J'ai rencontré de nombreuses hitbox invisible placées à des endroits aléatoire. Je suis même tombé sur un bug qui rendait mon écran complétement bleu et m'a obliger à relancer le jeu.  Pour conclure, Blasphemous est vraiment un jeu à essayer rien que pour découvrir son ambiance, ses musiques, ses graphismes et sa DA. Bien que je n'ai pas eu le courage de le 100%, j'ai apprécié les instants à plonger dans cet univers sombre et passionnant.   [quote] 7/10 [/quote]

OVERVIEW --Cons-- Dialect Endgame Completion  --Pros-- Achievements Amazing Story Amazing Graphics Smooth PVE  THOUGHTS  This game, like every metroidvania, has many things to do, which almost seems to be both good and bad. Collection and completion of the game requires you to play the campaign a multiple times in order to truly complete the game. The game also has a dialect of English(similar to Ole English) which you may not be familiar with, therefore u might need to search a few things to fully understand the story(A ingame glossary would have been a good addition). The story is amazing I've got no complains with how the pacing is crafted and the overall plot of the story. The connection of this game to theocracy creates this new image of Christianity and its different sides that were present in the middle ages. The graphics are insanely good, you can see the rough portrayal of emotions just like paintings in old time periods captured in pixel art. With how good the first iteration of this game, ill gladly grab Blasphemous 2!

[b]Un Pixel Art Gótico que te hace parir sangre.[/b] 🎭  Si andás con ganas de un [b]Metroidvania[/b] que no regala nada, [b]Blasphemous[/b] es una compra fija en Steam. El estudio español [b]The Game Kitchen[/b] y [b]TEAM17[/b] (Los de [b]Worms[/b]) se mandaron un laburo impresionante metiendo el folklore religioso de la Semana Santa andaluza en una licuadora gótica. El resultado es un juego que se siente como si [b]Dark Souls[/b] y [b]Castlevania[/b] se hubieran ido de caravana juntos. Acá sos el [b]Penitente[/b], un tipo con un cono de bronce en la cabeza que sale a repartir espadazos en un mundo maldito que se llama [b]Cvstodia[/b].  ✅[b]El Combate:[/b] rústico, pesado y quirúrgico, olvidate de la agilidad de [b]Hollow Knight[/b] o [b]Ori[/b]; acá el movimiento tiene inercia, se siente el peso del cuerpo y de la Mea Culpa (tu espada).  ✅[b]El ritmo:[/b] Al principio te puede parecer un poco tosco, pero es una decisión de diseño. No podés ir a lo loco a machacar botones porque los enemigos más petes te bajan de dos bifes.  ✅[b]Las mecánicas:[/b] La clave de la supervivencia es dominar el [b]Parry[/b] y el [b]Dash[/b] en el momento exacto. Cuando le agarrás la mano al [b]Timing[/b], el juego fluye que es un poema. Además, las ejecuciones sangrientas cuando dejás a un bicho mareado son un orgasmo visual.  ✅[b]El Mapa:[/b] Conectado mediante exploracion como buen [b]Metroidvania[/b], es un laberinto. Al principio vas medio perdido y el juego te deja elegir varios caminos, lo cual está genial porque si te trabás con un jefe podés ir a mutar a otra zona. El diseño de niveles es excelente, conectando zonas de una manera muy orgánica.  ❌[b]El Repaso:[/b] El viaje rápido escasea bastante al principio y te vas a comer varias caminatas largas por escenarios que ya limpiaste, lo que a veces te da un poco de paja. Lo bueno es que hay un montón de secretos, reliquias y paredes falsas que premian al que no es un ansioso de m... y se pone a explorar cada rincón.  ⭐️[b](89)[/b]

Jogo estilo metroidvania com um universo esplendido, confuso e cativante. Arte linda, cada cenário parecendo uma pintura renascentista esquecida pelo tempo... Gameplay padrão de um metroidvania, história escondida em seus itens e coletáveis, jogo bem linear com um ou outro puzzle mas nada de muito difícil.  Sua dublagem (principalmente em espanhol) é fenomenal, a dublagem juntamente da trilha sonora te proporcionam uma emersão incrível. Ponto chave de Blasphemous é sua história e gameplay cativante, você nunca se sente perdido ou fraco, sempre encontrando novos itens para exploração (o que é um fator replay muito legal) e melhorias de combate.  No final das contas, Blasphemous é um ótimo feijão com arroz que deixa o sentimento de "quero mais" para fãs de metroidvania.   Recomendo.

I hate to say it, but this game is not good. I really wanted to like it, it reeks of style and developer passion. The game's music and atmosphere are top notch, however, the game just falls flat in the most important aspects: it feels awful. The controls are clunky and combat is weak. Now, most people will say, "it is a metroidvania game, of course it feels awful at the start." Unfortunately, no. I have played and loved many metroidvania games, including the originals. This game's controls are pretty awful feeling, no screw attack or double jump upgrade will fix the way the game moves and feels; it just lacks fluidity. I really wanted to like this game and am still happy to support them on this passion project and hope they make a second one that builds on this one, but this one is not great.

Blasphemous é um jogo interessante e misturado de ideias que normalmente me agradariam, a direção de arte é incrível, os cenários e os design dos personagens e inimigos combinam bem com a proposta do jogo, a história é interessante, mas apesar de todos esses pontos positivos, Blasphemous se torna frustrante e cansativo muito rápido, existe claramente uma ótima ideia aqui, mas várias decisões de design acabam tornando a experiência mais entendiante do que divertida.  Inicialmente a gameplay até conseguiu me cativar, mas depois de algumas horas o jogo simplesmente deixou de ser divertido, várias vezes pensei ''quando essa área vai acabar?'' ou ''até quando vou enfrentar esse mesmo tipo de inimigo?'', e ao invés de continuar por empolgação, parecia que eu estava jogando apenas por obrigação.  Grande parte desse problema veio do combate e da movimentação, tudo em Blasphemous parece desajeitado,  os movimentos não são fluidos e passam uma sensação dura o tempo inteiro, os ataques não tem a suavidade que um jogo desse estilo precisa para poder evitar os inimigos.  Isso poderia até ser tolerável se o jogo compensasse em outros aspectos, mas ele conta com mecânicas mal polidas, como o dano de contato, que é provavelmente uma das decisões de design mais descuidadas do jogo, com praticamente todo inimigo causando uma quantia absurda de dano ao toque. Muito dos inimigos em Blasphemous já causam uma quantia de dano bem elevada com seus próprios ataques, então a adição desse dano insano ao contato só faz ser injusto ao invés de desafiador, a proposta é sobre criar dificuldade, mas ela se torna mais frustante do que desafiadora.  Também tem o problema de implementar um sistema mal polido de parry no jogo que não é eficaz contra a maioria dos inimigos, não há contra-ataque porque você é empurrado ou ignoram completamente a defesa e você toma dano do mesmo jeito, inutilizando completamente uma mecânica de combate essencial para um soulslike e servindo mais como enfeite.   É um jogo que me impressionou muito mais pela estética do que pela gameplay em si. Talvez eu até mude de ideia, pois pretendo jogar um pouco mais, mas no momento da análise não recomendo.

I overall enjoyed my time with Blasphemous, but some of this game felt infuriating and annoying rather than difficult. I didn't struggle on any bosses until the last 2 bosses, but man some maps are grueling to get through and genuinely feel poorly designed. The world itself is beautiful, the music is excellent and the art is top notch. All of this is dragged down by some baffling level design and enemy placement, along with a sprinkle of jank with platforming and the occassional bug causing either my character to get permanently stuck in a spot during a boss fight, or an enemy to get stuck in a locked door segment meaning I need to keep restarting.   The platforming occassionally felt like a massive drag. Lantern jumping honestly felt tacked on and poorly executed, all for a handful of sections and wasn't used in any meaningful way. Moving platform sections are not synched up correctly and seem to have an out of alignment cycle, meaning you might have to wait for a few loops of a moving platform for them to link up so you can jump across.   The sheer amount of deaths that felt cheap rather than actually deserved, like some just bafflingly placed enemies, or blind jumps having a spike pit at the bottom. There are a number of ladders that have an enemy placed to perfectly knock you off unless you bait out attacks every time (just plain annoying) along with other cheap gimmick enemy placements that were just frustrating rather than truly difficult.  Did ending B+C, I don't particularly feel like doing it again for ending A. Hoping Blasphemous 2 improves on these areas.  Over all a solid 7/10.

Blasphemous consumed my soul for hundreds of hours and I regret nothing.  Got all achievements, explored every corner, suffered through every boss fight, and honestly… this is one of the best Metroidvanias I’ve ever played.  The pixel art is unreal. Every area looks like a cursed painting, every enemy is horrifying in the best possible way, and the atmosphere never stops being oppressive and beautiful at the same time. The soundtrack perfectly matches the world and makes everything feel even more haunting.  Combat feels great, bosses are tough but satisfying, and exploration constantly rewards you with secrets, lore, and upgrades. Speaking of lore: the story is weird, tragic, confusing, and somehow amazing all at once.  This game doesn’t just have style — it has soul.  10/10. The miracle was painful. Would suffer again.

It's a very good game, I simply love pixel games. A friend had told me about this game and I didn't have high expectations, but let me tell you, this game shut me up. It's simply perfect. The story is very exciting since we are a penitent and we use mea culpa, and we only have one objective: to kill the priest because he lost his faith. So we have to bring his faith back by defeating him. But in the true ending, after defeating the priest, his two forms appear, but red, and it's very difficult, but after a few attempts we succeed. The ending is the penitent executing the three heads of the women we find in the area far below where the boss, Our Lady of the Scorched Face, is located. Regarding the true ending, I particularly love it because it has a cutscene showing something similar to a womb, and it's brilliant because it gives us a glimpse of what the second one will be like. But to summarize everything, I've already finished the first one from the Play Store and the second one from the Steamribbon site. Now, as a big fan, I've decided to buy Blasphemous 1 and I will also buy 2 because here's my reflection: We like something, so why simply try, or at least have it?

[b]Blasphemous[/b] delivers a unique atmosphere and excellent visuals, but it is hindered by rigid design choices and exploration that often feels more punishing than the boss fights themselves.  [h1]1. Exploration and Map[/h1] [b]+[/b] Excellent verticality and a thematic sense of oppression. [b]-[/b] Slog-like backtracking: fast travel is expensive and arrives too late. The 3-slot relic limit forces annoying inventory micromanagement. Map icons are unclear.  [h1]2. Combat and Arsenal[/h1] [b]+[/b] Responsive swordplay, rewarding parries, and precise enemy hitboxes. High build variety via Rosary Beads and Hearts. [b]-[/b] Sword upgrades past level 3 feel redundant. Environmental hitboxes (flames and spikes) can be imprecise.  [h1]3. Lore and NG+[/h1] [b]+[/b] Fascinating grotesque lore and 3 distinct endings. Excellent Boss Rushes and "Penitences" in NG+. [b]-[/b] Completionism and the Platinum trophy require multiple repetitive runs, favoring time investment over pure skill.  [h1]4. Characters and Quests[/h1] [b]+[/b] Memorable characters and deep, item-based storytelling. [b]-[/b] Frustrating quest design: invisible triggers and unknown timers can permanently lock content, forcing extra playthroughs.  [h1]5. Bosses and Performance[/h1] [b]+[/b] Visually spectacular bosses; top-tier pixel art and animations. Solid performance in the DLC-complete version. [b]-[/b] No native ultrawide support (mods compromise visual transitions).  [h1]Conclusion[/h1] A visceral experience that wins on aesthetics but suffers from dated quest mechanics. It lacks modern [b]QOL[/b] features, particularly regarding menu management and travel efficiency.

Плюсы:  - Шикарный красивейший и необычный пиксель-арт: от рядовых врагов и боссов до локаций и анимаций - Продуманные разнообразные локации с кучей секретов - Неплохая боевая система с использованием ульт, баффов и приемов - Отличный гипер запоминающийся оригинальный саундтрек - Супер необыкновенный лор и мир игры, а так же очень не дурный сюжет с квестами  Минусы:  - Нет двойного прыжка: вместо него есть костыль, когда нужно ударить в воздухе предмет, чтоб перепрыгнуть дальше - Местами кривейший платформер, с некорректно работающими лестницами, выступами и прочими деталями уровня - Чейн-демадж от атак врагов, боссов или локации: нет фрейма неуязвимости и героя кидает, как куклу пока по нему наносят урон без возможности отпрыгнуть или отблокировать - Нет возможности вставить Все улучшения для изучения мира сразу: по не понятным причинам возможно вставить только 3 и в зависимости от головоломки нужно менять через паузу, а таких ситуаций несколько на 20 часов игры  На 2026 год игра находится в самом лучшем ее виде: с возможностью открыть истинную концовку C, ставить метки на карте, появился гоуст джамп, чтоб было удобнее прыгать, легче стало цепляться за уступы и в целом навалили очень много контента. Однозначно одна из самых крутых стильных и мясных метроидваний, сильная проба пера разработчика и прекрасный задел на сиквел, в котором сделано уже все на голову лучше.

You know, in a weird way this game really made me appreciate my faith in Christ even more lol. The predominant theme in this game is suffering, all to atone for your sins and appeal to the whims of some capricious, eldritch force called the Miracle. No matter what you do, though, your absolution is never guaranteed, and whether you do receive absolution or punishment, the end result is the same: more suffering, more pleading, never certain whether "enough" is enough, only watching as you deteriorate along with the rest of the world subject to the same cruel force.   An enshrinement of transactional suffering and worship of a force that ultimately doesn't really seem to care about you.  Not so with Christ, who suffered for us so that we might not have to suffer, and who through His death and resurrection, guaranteed the atonement of our sins so that we might be reconciled to Him, a God who truly does care about us, hears us, and desires the best for us.   Theological musings aside, though, this is a great game. I've never played too many Metroidvanias—my main one is Castlevania: Symphony of the Night, which sets a pretty high bar that Blasphemous is able to meet. The environmental imagery is grotesque in many ways but still evocative and beautiful due to its inspiration from Catholic Christian imagery and architecture. Each area successfully manages to create a feeling of foreboding, sorrow, reverence, and even adventure, due in no small part to the music. The music is definitely one of my favorite things about the game, and it really made me appreciate traditional Spanish music... something that's a bit personally relevant to me after growing up being exposed to Hispanic music styles like salsa, reggaeton, and norteña that I largely found obnoxious and grating.   Obviously, music is largely a matter of taste, but it really was nice finally being exposed to a Hispanic musical style I actually really liked.  Being a Spanish speaker, I set the language (audio and text) to Spanish and was impressed by how good the voice acting was and how many new, if potentially archaic, words I learned lol. Even if you don't speak Spanish, you should definitely set it to Spanish still and just have the text in English, because the voice acting really does a lot for immersion and creating that feeling of being in an old, noble, and "foreign" place.  Finally, the gameplay. Movement is simplistic in design since it is a 2D game, but it doesn't feel clunky or outdated and instead feels smooth and responsive. Combat, mainly when it comes to the bosses, is challenging but fair, and it was fun figuring out their movesets to finally get a clean (clean enough at least lol) win in.  My main advice when it comes to exploration would be to make good use of the map marking system, because there will be PLENTY of areas and items that are inaccessible until much later in the game once you've unlocked  the necessary powerup to reach them. It's a fairly large map, too, so don't count on yourself to remember exactly what you missed and where.

I bounced off this twice previously, I can't really remember why, I assume I was approaching it wrong. This is really Dark Souls 1 tier stuff, thoroughly rewards patient, careful play and savagely punishes being a ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ with marathon bonfire run to get back to where you were.  But, it's good. A sort of catholic, very Spanish, 2D Bloodborne. All of the lore is sort-of just catholic ceremony and ritual but given a 10% extra twist, look at the background of the wound kissing convent, the animations, think about what Wound Kissing actually entails. It stays with you!  The voice acting is charmingly non-professional, or maybe it's the audio recording? It all just feels a bit... off, but in a very endearing way. I'm going to keep going with this and see how far I can get, it's actually good, folks!

Blasphemous is one of the most uniquely atmospheric metroidvanias ever made. The Spanish Catholic horror aesthetic is unlike anything else in the genre, dripping with grotesque religious imagery, tortured saints, and bleeding statues that all somehow look gorgeous in pixel art form. The combat is brutal and deliberate, the world of Cvstodia is haunting in the best way, and the lore goes absurdly deep if you're willing to dig for it. The Game Kitchen carved out their own corner of the genre with this one, and even years later there's nothing else quite like it.

セールで購入したが全実績解除まで楽しく遊べたので２Dアクションゲーム好きにはオススメ！ ドット絵は血飛沫ドバドバだけど綺麗で動きも滑らか。 難易度は慣れるまで理不尽に思えるが、対処法が用意されているので少し難しい程度。 二周目をクリアしてから新規データで始めると簡単に感じたぐらいゲームに慣れるのが大事。 敵の攻撃パターンを覚えて対処していくのは楽しかった。  初回クリアまでは攻略サイトを見ずに進めたら結構な時間がかかってしまった。 １．回避中は完全無敵と思っていた（無敵はあるけど短い、高速移動と考えた方がいい） ２．高いところにあるアイテムは二段ジャンプでとると思っていた（エア・インパルスでOKだった） ３．ロザリオに毒効果軽減を付けて毒霧の中を突っ切った（敵からの毒効果軽減だった） ４．ソコロの苦しみに終焉をもたらしてしまった（ティエント貰えず） ５．教会への寄付を後回しにしてお金が余るまでしなかった（ワープとかMP回復とか便利）  二周目は敵が強化されすぎていて実績解除には不向きだった。 強くてニューゲームが良かったなぁと個人的には思う。

[i]Blasphemous[/i] isn't a game for everyone. It features heavy gore, some nudity, and deeply disturbing creature designs that may not sit well with all players. However, if you don't mind, the game offers a truly captivating experience.  The soundtrack is beautiful, perfectly complementing it's dark & religious atmosphere. The art is incredible (detailed & unique). The gameplay feels satisfying and rewarding.  The boss difficulty is challenging but not overly stressful as long as you progress properly. Avoid jumping into areas too early, and make sure to hit a wall every map to discover a hidden area (this is the key). Upgrade mea culpa, bile flasks, and maximum health. With the right preparation, the game strikes a solid balance between challenge and fairness.

Cara que jogo bom, papo reto...  Eu sempre me senti intimidado com metroidvanias no geral. Tentei jogar Hollow Knight fiquei super perdido e acabei droppando, tentei jogar Symphony of the Night e mesmo achando um puta jogo também não tive vontade de zerar o game. Sempre achei q não era um gênero pra mim, um estilo de jogo pra quem tem mais senso de direção e paciência de ficar fazendo backtracking e testando múltiplos caminhos. Mas depois de zerar Blasphemous essa ideia que eu tive caiu por terra completamente.  Sim, eu fiquei perdido 90% do tempo, sem nem ao menos saber onde eu fui e deixei de ir. Foi difícil em vários momentos, descobrir o que fazer a seguir, matar alguns bosses mais complicados ou até mesmo lidar com inimigos mais desafiadores. Tudo que eu tinha medo aconteceu hora ou outra jogando. Mas tudo isso fez parte da experiência e tornou a sensação de zerar o jogo inteiro sem olhar nada na internet, algo espetacular.  A arte de Blasphemous é linda, a trilha sonora é excelente, o level design é absurdamente bem construído, tem uma diversidade enorme de inimigos diferentes com movesets diferentes. É difícil encontrar algum problema ou defeito nesse jogo, sério mesmo. Não só foi um jogo incrível como fez eu ter vontade de jogar outros metroidvanias muito populares só pra ter a mesma sensação de exploração q eu tive jogando Blasphemous.  Agora pretendo continuar jogando mesmo depois de zerar pra, não só, pegar os outros finais mas como encontrar os outros segredos do mapa e quem sabe um dia fazer o 100%.

Si eres español (de españa, obvio) y te gustan los juegos del género metroidvania, Blasphemous es tu juego.  Aparte del cómo se emplea el habla en este juego y las MUY buenas voces españolas que hay, tiene un arte increíblemente bueno -aunque obviamente no sea la gran cosa a nivel gráfico- y, sumado a la ambientación y banda sonora, hacen de tu gameplay una experiencia bastante llevadera. Este juego, al igual que algunos otros de su género, tiene un muy buen sistema de progresión, siendo satisfactorio, pero lo que más llama la atención son los secretos y detalles que oculta; por ejemplo, hay un objeto -el cual no spoilearé- que LITERALMENTE no hace nada, a menos que mueras unas cuantas veces teniéndolo equipado, cambiando su descripción y aspecto conforme evoluciona. Podrá no ser algo que suene increíble, pero estos pequeños detalles te causan una cierta sensación de misterio y/o euforia -o al menos eso es lo que me pasa a mí-.  Otra cosa que me fascina -aunque por desgracia no me entero si no lo busco por YT u otras fuentes- son las tremendas inspiraciones que tiene este juego en Sevilla, ya sea por nombres, escenarios, acento, etc.   Siendo un jugador el cual ha jugado a varios juegos metroidvania como ambos Ori's ("Ori and the Blind Forest" y "Ori and the Will of the Wisps"), el Hollow Knight: Silksong (casi platinado a fecha de esta publicación) y el Hollow Knight (platinado en PS4 y aquí, Steam), puedo decir con total sinceridad que este juego, a pesar de no estar a la altura de estos últimos 2 titanes, sigue siendo un buen juego con un carisma único.

As with many games of this genre, there were moments where all I was doing was constant backtracking. I do plan to pick this back up in the future to try and achieve the other endings. Highly recommend if this game already seems up your alley, otherwise wait for a small sale to try it out.

Uno de mis metroidvania favoritos. Su pixel art está brutalmente bien logrado, con escenarios y diseños que transmiten una atmósfera oscura y única de principio a fin. La historia, aunque algo críptica, logra atraparte gracias a su gran construcción de mundo y todo el simbolismo que maneja.  El combate se siente muy bien logrado, la exploración recompensa bastante y los jefes destacan mucho tanto por diseño como por dificultad. Además, tiene muy buena rejugabilidad gracias a sus secretos, diferentes finales y todo el contenido opcional que invita a volver para descubrir más. Una experiencia totalmente increíble.

Буду честен. Долгое время относился к этой игре очень положительно. Изначально, в памяти она сохранилась как очень хорошая игра! Потом... Я прошёл Blasphemous 2 и после я не могу смотреть на первую часть нормально. Почти во всём, вторая часть выигрывает! Геймплей, графика, баланс... и т.д. Кроме парочки вещей (Правда это чистое имхо), но об этом позже, а пока: 1. Механика предметов, бусин, апгрейдов и исследование мира - Мне не очень нравятся. Бусины например очень неинтересные, а кроме них другие элементы вообще не выделяются. Предметы для исследования можно взять всего 3 штуки! Вопрос: А в чём смысл? Ведь их можно менять прямо во время игры. Тогда проще было их сделать перманентными апгрейдами, а не копаться в меню, чтобы их поменять!  Также есть такие себе сердцевины меча Mea Culpa! Как по мне вообще неинтересные! А насчёт исследования мира - есть ли смысл его изучать? Естественно есть! А интересно-ли? НЕТ! ВООБЩЕ НЕТ! Апгрейды на меч и на алтари например происходит через покупку улучшений за валюту, а не через исследование, как во второй части! Всё остальное в мире имеет мало смысла. Малыши по факты дают только предмет для исследования (названия для данных предметов не помню и вспоминать не хочу). А кости только валюту и проход к одному из боссов. 2. Некоторые решения в геймплее - Самая популярная проблема - это перманентная смерть на шипах или падения в яму - это позор! Сам персонаж ощущается так себе. Часть анимаций, особенно казней дико затянутые! Также часть атак дико неудобные, например Выпад вниз (тупое r0BH0). И это только то, что я смог вспомнить!  А плюсы есть? да, есть!  1. Визуальный стиль - он очень классный! Каждый персонаж, враг, локация и т.д. на что-то да отсылаются. То на искусство, к примеру очень много отсылок к испанским художникам и их работам, особенно на Франсиско Гойю. Также много отсылок на библию (зная, откуда создатели даже не удивлён). На удивление нашлось даже парочку на что-то современное! (сами ищите :) ). Сама игра отрисована очень недурно. Анимации хорошие (но во второй лучше!) Также сама атмосфера прямо завораживает, несмотря на жестокость этого мира.  2. (имхо лучше чем во второй части!) Музыка - Тут вообще без жалоб, музыка классная и атмосферная. Во второй по ощущениям она слабее!  Но как по мне этих плюсов не хватает, чтобы как-то оценить её выше.  Весь обзор чистое имхо.
